desc:
A tall cliffside rises to the west, the firm ground holding up well against the
wear of ages. A few mallorn trees grow here, hardly tall enough to reach halfway
to the cliff top, though still spectacularly well-grown compared to any normal
tree. Luxurious grass tickles the side of the cliff and the heels of the
mellyrn, its green prominence invading all space among them.
name: Under the Cliff

desc:
This clearing in the forest of Lothlorien gives way to a cliff that overlooks a
good part of the Naith below. Far away to the east, the fair city of Caras
Galadhon may be descried by none but the keenest eye. The ground falls away
sharply to the east. To the west, the clearing continues.
name: Clearing at a Cliff

desc:
Beautiful mellyrn are all the trees that grow here, their kingly golden crowns,
each one of a kind, glittering with every gust of the wind. Their grey trunks
are elven spears like none others, pointing into the sky defensively as if to
protect this enchanted land from whatever may attack it from above. A tall hill
rises to the southeast of here. From this side it looks unclimbable.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
Polished grey boles of the many mallorn trees that grow here dazzle the eye,
while their golden branches enchant with their beauty. The arms of the mallorn
trees rise high into the sky, bow-shaped, as if to cradle it in their caring
hands. A few snowflakes of forest moss sprinkle the ground along with an
occasional golden or silver star-shaped flower.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
The land drops sharply here, plummeting a hundred feet down an overgrown
forested incline before coming to rest in some thick sage bushes at the foot of
the hill. A few undersized mellyrn struggle to stand straight, their roots
plunging deep into the hillside. Fanuidhol stands out in the distance, the tall
white spur jutting into the clouds surrounding it like a wizard's hat.
name: Steeply Sloping Forest

desc:
The mallorn trees, each holding a piece of the soul of Lothlorien, are
prominently exhibited here in all their glory and splendour. Light is playing on
their silver trunks creating an illusion of water running down them. Like first
winter frost, the ground is touched by a silver web of snowflake-shaped moss
spread out close to the trees themselves. A half-domed hill rises to the north
of here.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
The forest floor takes an abrupt turn downward to the east, making travel in
that direction unwise. The valley below stretches out before you, but there are
better vantage points of the valley to the north of here, where the incline
turns into a cliff.
name: Rolling Forest

desc:
The land slopes down eastwards from here at a gentle angle, not impeding the
growth of the mallorn trees, but making their roots grow in a more spread out
fashion. The cosy places among the roots and just under the grey trunks make for
excellent hiding places for small forest animals. The golden forest stretches
out in all directions. To the north a small clearing is visible.
name: Rolling Forest

desc:
Upwards and onwards, the mallorn trees strive to reach the sky and to embrace
their brethren at the same time, their thin elegant arms holding their many
hands in a mock parody of a universal handshake. Star-shaped silver moss
conceals the soft brown ground here. The golden forest parts to the north.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
Trampled grass, so unusual for this land, strewn with flowers and thick
dishevelled sage bushes grows under a lone sprawling mallorn tree. Weighed down
by a flet, the ancient tree seems to have grown wide out and around as if to
cradle it within its mighty paws. An elven ladder is hanging along the trunk of
the tree. A sturdy swing made of the same kind of rope as the ladder is attached
to one of the stronger branches.
name: A Lonely Mallorn Tree

desc:
A steep incline levels out here. This part of the forest is destined to spend
most of its days in shadow, as the hill to the east obscures it from the sunrise
and Misty Mountains conceal it from the sunset. The golden forest is relatively
sparse here with little underbrush to hamper the passage and plenty of animal
tracks criss-crossing the ground each and every way.
name: In the Shadow of a Hill

desc:
This forest refuge is the abode of many forest animals. The soft ground is dug
up in many places between the roots of the famed mallorn trees. Their burnished
crowns dazzle with their exuberance. Mounds of yellow leaves are piled up
against the glossy silvery trunks. A fairly tall hill is silhouetted against the
sky eastwards.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
A collapsed tree in the middle of the clearing houses several mushroom patches.
Red, brown, purple and yellow, the mushroom caps come in all shapes and sizes -
a merry variety to go with the golden petals of elanor and gossamer stars of
niphredil that grow nearby. All around, the golden forest rises, as if
protecting the peace of this small pastoral corner of the world.
name: A Mushroom Field

desc:
The forest rolls downhill to the east here, continuing on an incline that began
somewhere to the west. Leaves and blooms from previous springs litter the forest
floor. An uncautious traveller could slip and find himself sliding down this
forested slope.
name: Rolling Forest

desc:
This part of the forest presents a picture that is quite unusual for the Golden
Wood: one of the mellyrn here seems to be dying. The rocky soil by the cliff
does not seem to be providing enough sustenance for the graceful tree, and its
once golden boughs and silvery trunk have turned a sickly yellow and dirty
brown.
name: A Failing Mallorn

desc:
The magnificent mallorn trees rise high here, as if pasted into this landscape
from some ancient elven tale of fame and glory. Destined to guard this land
until the last of elven race has passed away beyond the confines of this world,
they cow lesser trees with their graceful outline and noble stature. A hill
rises to the north of here. In all other directions the forest grows even
deeper.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
A nearly vertical hillside rises to the north right in front of a small open
space closely surrounded by an ancient forest of tall gold-crowned trees. Two
strands of the same ivy bush, its leaves a deep luscious green, run up the
hillside parting slightly and then meeting up again where they end, forming a
circle. The top of the hill is barely visible, but some switchbacks run up the
side of it from some spot to the east.
name: At the Bottom of the Hill

desc:
Sporadic, cheerily green vegetation dominates the landscape here. Sage and berry
bushes, and the flowers are enjoying unrestricted access to the sun as very few
trees grow here. The few that do are young mellyrn, their silvery trunks and
golden crowns benefiting greatly from the contrast with the green background of
the forested hillside to the south.
name: Lightly Forested Foothills

desc:
The ground slopes downwards to the north at a rather uncomfortable angle, but
that does not seem to hinder the trees of the golden forest. Mighty mellyrn, all
of them, the trees seem to penetrate the skies with their tops and illuminate it
with the fine web of their golden branches glinting merrily at any time of the
day, whether in the light of Sun, or Moon, or stars. A hill rises to the south
of here, though it looks too steep to climb from this side.
name: Sloping Golden Forest

desc:
The glade of tall springy grass is surrounded by the golden forest. The ground
is even here, and but for the surrounding trees this opening would look
perfectly ordinary. The vegetation looks forever undisturbed, though whether
this effect is due to little traffic through this area or to some special magic
worked by the Lady of the Land, one cannot say. A tall stone block with flat
weathered surface is standing upright here.
name: Peaceful Glade

desc:
The dense mallorn forest borders the apple orchard of Lady Galadriel from the
north. The tall noble trees garbed in silver and gold provide a marked contrast
to the greenery of the garden. Basil grows in abundance here, clutching at the
ivy-strung fence. A few young apple offshoots reach out and up, having escaped
the confines of the planted garden.
name: Forest behind the Orchard Fence

desc:
The breathtaking beauty of the mallorn forest does not yield to passing of age
or Men. This corner of the land seems frozen in time, ages ago, when no word was
yet laid on stream or stone. Lack of young trees or grass on the firm
golden-brown ground complements this ageless effect.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
Silvery trees surround a small glade strung with ivy bushes. Their long threads,
thickly dotted with dark green leaves, cover the ground in several layers as
they do not have anything to hang on to, and it seems as if some power is
preventing them from venturing into the golden forest around. A small stone
plaque is barely concealed under the live tangle of ivy and fallen golden
leaves.
name: Ivy Meadow within the Golden Wood

desc:
A forest of mighty mellyrn, wide of girth and tall of height, of indeterminate
age grows here. The whispering of their golden-tipped branches in the wind
mingles with the singing of birds. The grass is short though luxurious here, and
among its healthy greenery, a few flowers sparkle, indigenous to Lorien. An
intangible air of mystery fills the forest, as if it is always on the verge of
some great happenstance.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
Moving swiftly among the proud mellyrn, this small but well-trodden path runs
winding and twisting beneath the great waving branches, darting in and out
between the silvery grey trunks. Soft rustling can be heard as well as the
peaceful chirping of birds from within the forest.
name: A Small Trail

desc:
This small stream courses southwards amongst the great trees that surround it,
babbling happily and sending joyful echoes into the dense wood beyond. Just
alongside the stream lies a small forest path, opening up the forest floor to
the skies above and allowing some light to fall upon the otherwise shadowy
ground.
name: A Babbling Stream

desc:
The surrounding forest has drawn back respectfully here, making room for this
single, great mallorn tree. The girth of this grey-clad giant strikes you with
awe, and its heavily laden boughs stretch both far and wide to catch the light
of sun or moon upon its countless clusters of saffron-shaded blossoms in summer
and gilted leaves in winter. From within the grey maze of its branches the
cheerful warbling of songbirds can be heard, and the wind whispers softly as it
gently caresses the ancient tree.
name: An Ancient Mallorn

desc:
The path makes a wide turn across a grassy boulder-strewn clearing in which a
few mallorn trees grow. It is high up the southern slope of the hill. The wind
gently ruffles the leaves and branches of the few mellyrn that grow here. Golden
patches of elanor are clustered around the rocks trying to escape the occasional
strong gusts of the winds that threaten to send them floating down into the
Golden Wood proper. The path disappears into the forest south of here, while its
western end launches into the final ascent.
name: Lightly Forested Clearing

desc:
A narrow, barely trodden path runs up the southern slope of a tall hill. The
mallorn trees that grow here seem to be executing an eternal southward-bound
bow. The silvery trees up the slope shelter the path from the sun with their
golden crowns even as it goes into several sharp switchbacks as it ascends. The
switchbacks lengthen the climb but make it less strenuous, and small rocks guide
the traveller's way.
name: Switchbacks

desc:
The main pathway makes a wide turn here coming in from the south and turning
east. The exquisite silver-green canvas of the sage brush skirts wide of the
turn, giving way to a half-moon of granite rocks set in the ground to the south
of the path. To the north, a hill rises swiftly, its steepness hidden by the
young mallorn forest and more sage underbrush. A tiny path disappears up the
hill and turns west as its goes into its first switchback.
name: Fork at the Foot of the Hill

desc:
The trail runs from the east to the west here, passing just south of a small
waterfall that sends the light reflected from its cascading surface to shimmer
and sparkle among the towering mellyrn. Surrounded by the softly whispering
forest, you hear also the joyous babbling of the small brook as it runs swiftly
alongside the forest path.
name: A Forest Path

desc:
Babbling happily, the small stream runs among the roots of the solemn trees
towering above. As it courses down a small hill, the water is sent cascading
over the mossy stones, creating a small and beautiful waterfall which sparkles
in the soft light. A little pool has gathered below the falls, offering the
thirsty wanderer a cool and refreshing drink.
name: A Small Waterfall

desc:
Flowing gently southward, the stream winds and twists as it darts in and out
between the towering grey mellyrn. The clear and cold water sparkles as the
light cast by the skies above penetrates the foliage, creating dancing patterns
of green and gold. The golden woods whisper softly as a small breeze passes by.
From a distance, the song of the nightingales can be heard.
name: Small Stream

desc:
Twisting and turning gently, the stream winds its way between the solemn grey
trees, reflecting what little light filters down through the golden canopy
above. The soft babbling of the water pouring over the mossy stones mingles with
the whispers of the wind in the branches and the soft singing of distant
nightingales.
name: A Small Stream

desc:
The forest opens up and the tall mellyrn graciously allow the light of the sky
to fall upon the well-travelled path. Running almost in a straight line, the
narrow trail runs parallel to a small forest stream. Accompanied by the babbling
of the brook and the song of the hidden birds, the trail continues north and
south.
name: Forest Trail

desc:
A light clear path emerges from the mallorn forest to the south. It widens
considerably as it turns west and dives under the shadow of the aspen gate that
leads to the apple orchard beyond. The ground is grassless here but firm, and
the grey mellyrn provide all the shelter one might need from rain, fog, and
unfriendly eyes that always watch the land of Lorien from afar. Glimpses of
another trail can be seen to the east.
name: A Path in the Golden Wood

desc:
The green branches of four slender aspen trees, standing in a square, entwine
forming a thick canopy that shelters the entrance to the apple orchard. A fence
of ivy coming up to it from north and south weaves itself around the aspens, so
that the only way in is to walk between them. These trees, though dwarfed by the
mellyrn of the Golden Wood, are still by far some of the tallest trees to
inhabit Middle-earth.
name: Entrance to the Orchard

desc:
The apple orchard of Lady Galadriel would be an object of envy for any Shire
farmer. Apple trees of all shapes and sizes, most of them in bloom, have the run
of the place. Ripe apples hang like oversized decorations in this carnival of
green and pink, green and gold, green and the most magnificent royal red. The
orchard is surrounded by a fence of ivy, separating it from the mallorn forest
outside. The apple trees hang low and heavy under the weight of their fruits.
The power of this land is evident even here, for the apple trees of Lorien are
the size of grown oaks, if not quite making it close to that of the mellyrn.
name: The Apple Orchard

desc:
A faint forest footpath, outlined with tiny red and golden flowers and white
polished pebbles, widens slightly and then comes to a dead stop here, as it
comes against a fence of ivy, strung among some bushes and aspen trees. An apple
orchard lies beyond the fence to the east. Slightly darkened silvery trunks of
the mallorn trees and their outstretched golden crowns conceal this place from
the rest of the world.
name: End of a Forest Path

desc:
The pebbled elven footpath enters from the south here, tracing the foot of a
tall hill outlined against the western sky, and then diving into the lustrous
golden forest in the east. The passage is level, strewn with pebbles and larger
polished stones along the sides, and no tracks can be distinguished upon them.
Exuberant, elanor glitters in the grass, as if a hundred golden eyes are winking
at you mischievously.
name: Bend in the Elven Footpath

desc:
A minor footpath hugs the foothills here. Ascending steeply, the hill rises to
the west, shielding travellers from the gloomy view of Misty Mountains. Barely
visible, the switchbacks zigzag from somewhere to the east up the hill and out
of the view. Golden flowers glint in the grass just below the silvery trunks of
the mallorn forest stretching for miles and miles to the east.
name: Faint Footpath along the Foot of the Hill

desc:
A lethargic path runs around the foot of the hill here. The golden forest rises
to the east of it. Some lively raspberry bushes intermixed with nettles line the
slope to the west, looking very out of place in their down-to-earth simplicity.
An occasional pebble tumbles downhill and lands on the byway.
name: A Faint Footpath

desc:
Quaint and lordly, the ancient mallorn trees detach themselves from the dreamy
curtain of the darkened enchanted forest. They rise high, their sheer grey
branches silhouetted ghostlike against the sky. The golden forest is thick here,
so that hardly any ground can be seen among the sturdy mallorn roots that dig
their fingers deep into the ground to support the giant grey trunks.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
Deep shadows line the naked ground sprinkled with dead golden leaves. But they
are not the scary shadows of morbid children's tales, but the warm shadows of
late summer evenings. By some fluke of heavenly design, mellyrn have grown
especially close to each other in this part of the forest. The branches of their
crowns are interwoven with each other as to make each particular one
indistinguishable, creating a near perfect illusion of isolation from the rest
of the world.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
Deep shadows line the ground here. Among the ethereal splendour of the mallorn
wood, all other things seem diminished. The light hardly touches their silver
trunks, yet their shadows are many and their outlines are clearly visible. The
interplay of light reveals that the bark pattern on the trees is not always
natural. Thick though unthreatening, the golden forest stretches out for miles
in all directions.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
The smooth forest ground rises eastward here. The grey mallorn trees of great
height and mighty girth populate the slope. Their gold-covered branches are
concentrated on the top parts of the upward narrowing trunks, but they are too
thin and delicate to conceal the sky, the magnificent westward view of the gaunt
grey peaks of the Misty Mountains, and the fact that the golden forest stretches
far in all directions.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
Sparsely growing mallorn trees dot the landscape. Eastwards a faint path can be
seen through their silver filigree. The tall proud trees have especially
sprawling golden crowns, but the shadows they cast are still not as deep as the
ones in the deeper parts of the elven forest. The ground turns into white rock
and drops off sharply to the south, where a view opens onto the southern parts
of this enchanted place.
name: Light Golden Forest

desc:
Ghostly light illuminates the grey-green grass, the sage bushes, and the lower
parts of the mallorn trees. Silver diaphanous stars of niphredil sparkle around
in abundance. The magical forest feels alive with power. Some more green is
visible through the trees to the north, and a path can be seen, running to the
east of here.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
The mellyrn, their branches elegant and sprawling, are watching the forest on
either side of the path, of which the only indicator is this curious disregard.
This widening in the woods is due not so much to the trees growing sparsely, as
it is due to the branches of the mallorn trees diverting most of their attention
away from the path. A subtle sense of consciousness emanates from the trees as
if all the ghosts of all the elven legends of the Golden Wood became whispering
shadows living hidden within those majestic grey trees.
name: A Faint Path through the Golden Wood

desc:
Bordered by tall and proud mellyrn, the trail is partly shadowed here as it
curves gently southward, following the forest stream. All around you are the
soft sounds of the living forest of Lothlorien.
name: A Forest Trail

desc:
Running alongside the small babbling stream, this well-trodden path is shadowed
by the tall trees standing densely all around. The trail curves slightly here
from west to south, keeping up with the flowing water. The soft trickling of the
stream becomes intertwined with the rustling of the trees and the song of
concealed birds.
name: A Path through the Forest

desc:
Turning slowly, the forest path follows the small stream carefully on its course
towards the Celebrant, forming a border between the silver sheen of the water
and the golden woods. From within the trees, a soft whispering can be heard as
well as the warbling of unseen songbirds.
name: A Forest Path

desc:
The trees slowly open up, making room for a small trail just north and east of
here. As the woods grow less dense, the shadows are dispelled by the light from
the sky falling almost unhindered towards the ground. Within the few remaining
shadows, faint moving shapes can be seen and soft singing can be heard.
name: Forest Bordering the Trail

desc:
A faint snake of pebbles slithers southward among the silvery trunks of mallorn
trees and down a mild incline. The only indication that this is indeed a path
and not a fluke of forest magic is that the pebble trace widens to the south and
is not longer concealed by the golden boughs of nearby mellyrn. The path is just
as faint to the north as it disappears among the mallorn trees and into the
forest beyond.
name: A Faint Path through the Golden Wood

desc:
Stopping by a flowering shrub here, turning at a cluster of bright flowers
there, this small trail runs through the forest like a wanderer following
somebody's light footsteps or a voice calling at the edge of hearing. Here and
there it approaches tall mallorn trees whose branches hold flets and birds'
nests, yet nowhere does it stop to rest at the trunk of a tree or wait for a
ladder to be thrown from the canopy. The chirping of birds can be heard from the
shrubs and trees around the trail, from whence they observe the woodland path
unseen.
name: A Small Trail

desc:
Flowing out of a small depression to the north, the stream picks up speed as it
hurries down the sloping ground south on its way to meet Celebrant. The
streambed is sandy, but the water is clear and sparkling, as if it had never
touched sand nor forest soil. Mellyrn bend to the stream from the soft grassy
banks, their reflection a blur of silver and gold in the fast flowing water.
name: A Stream in the Golden Wood

desc:
Two layers of stones have been laid on the ground here, forming a simple hearth,
on which a small fire is burning. The stones have obviously been arranged very
carefully, to prevent the fire from breaking out of its assigned borders and
singeing the live soil and grass of the forest. Since here, in the very heart of
Lorien, Elves have little need of light in the dark or heat in the Winter, they
probably use this fire to bake apples and toast bread, and in the night tell
stories and sing songs around it, watching the sparks fly into the dark sky.
name: By a Fire

desc:
The forest opens up here into a large clearing, almost two hundred feet across.
It is circular in shape and one can only guess at the reason why. Perhaps as
night falls and the stars shine brightly above, the elves light fires, dance and
sing, celebrating and enjoying themselves. There are remnants of a fire in the
centre, and you can see that the grass is lying flat in a small circle around
the hearth. From the surrounding forest you can hear song and laughter as well
as the soft whispering of the enchanted forest.
name: A Forest Clearing

desc:
Standing tall and solemn, this proud forest strives towards the sky, forever
trying to reach the twinkling stars that bathe Lothlorien in their silver light
during nighttime. Standing rather far apart, the green and golden crowns do not
prevent the light from the sky from reaching the ground and dispelling the grey
and green shadows that rest from time to time among the grey trunks. The woods
sway softly to the occasional passing gust of wind, which creates a faint song
among the branches and leaves, mingling with the distant song of birds.
name: Proud Forest

desc:
As the small stream gathers momentum, it can be heard clearly from the thick
surrounding forest. Clear and cold, the water strives eastward towards its long
sought for companion. Running side by side, the stream seems to be in friendly
competition with the forest path.
name: A Rapid Stream

desc:
Running alongside the swift forest stream, this small path lies below the open
sky. The great trees of the Golden Wood stand sparse here, allowing the light of
the sky to fall unhindered upon the well-trodden soil. The soft splashing from
the stream blends with the song of hidden nightingales and the whisper of the
mellyrn as the wind gently rocks the forest.
name: A Forest Path

desc:
The road turns from the north to the west, following the brink of a deep fosse.
The sides of the fosse are almost vertical and covered in grass, while its
bottom is concealed in green dusk. Upon the distant side of the fosse there
rises to a great height a green wall encircling a grassy hill thronged with
mallorn trees taller than any of the trees in the forest. Their height cannot be
guessed, but they stand like living towers, and amid their branches countless
lights are gleaming, green and gold and silver.
name: Before the Walls of Caras Galadhon

desc:
Two forest roads end here at the base of a white stone staircase leading up to
the paved road that encircles the green walls of the fair Caras Galadhon. The
white stairs are sprinkled with red, green, blue and silvery sparks which
glitter like the jewelled dust that, according to the legends of the First Age,
sparkled on the quays of Alqualonde and the stairs of Tirion upon Tuna.
name: On the White Steps

desc:
Two winding lines of round white stones trace the southern and the northern
edges of the grass-covered road. White and blue flowers grow between and around
the stones in a seemingly random pattern, spots of blue scattered among the
spots of white as if the flowers were playing tag. The golden trees that grow on
both sides of the road seem to stay at a respectful distance from the lines of
stones, as if unwilling to disrupt the work of an unknown gardener or architect.
name: A Road through the Golden Wood

desc:
Golden trees stand densely to the north and the south of the road that turns
from the east to the north, skirting a fern-covered slope to the west. Like
stars or tiny flowers, bright sparks of different colours, red, purple, blue and
yellow, seem to glimmer here and there among the ferns on the slope, blinking
out of existence when one tries to examine them. Whether this is an illusion, or
actual glimpses of something hidden beneath the fronds, or minute displays of a
magic innate to these great green plants, the elves that dwell in the Golden
Wood might know, but a mortal eye cannot tell.
name: At a Bend in the Forest Road

desc:
Running down to the road that crosses the forest to the north and east, this
slope is entirely covered with ferns. There are many different types of ferns
here, some of their fronds lacy, some splitting into sickle-shaped, round, oval
or sword-like leaves. These leaves range in colour from light green, as if
bleached by the sun, to the dark green of evergreen trees. No ground can be
distinguished below the ferns, and of all the plants only trees rise above them,
their leaves high enough to gather the sunlight before it is lost and absorbed
by this green sea. If anyone were to drop something on this slope, it might
never be found unless the landscape changed completely, and the ferns were
driven away.
name: On a Fern-covered Slope

desc:
Northwards the majestic golden forest stretches across the horizon, but to the
south a low grassy slope rises from the ground. At its top, a white paved road
runs by the foot of the green walls of Caras Galadhon, almost in parallel to the
forest road but slightly above. At some distance in the east the two roads
converge at a white staircase which seems to glitter from afar; the stone road
reaches the top of the stair, and the grassy road reaches its bottom.
name: A Road through the Golden Wood

desc:
Among the great grey trees, several elves of the Galadhrim have built their
homes. Living high up in the trees upon wooden platforms, they work in harmony
with the surrounding forest. Close to your west, the woods open up into a small
clearing, but in all other directions the forest is dense and lies in deep
shadows.
name: Forest Dwellings

desc:
Here in the heart of Lothlorien the proud mellyrn stand tall. The mighty boughs
support a few wooden platforms nearly hidden among the branches. A few ladders
stand against the grey trunks of the trees, allowing access to the lofty homes
of the Galadhrim. A little bit away from the populated trees a large number of
beehives stand in a small cluster of buzzing activity.
name: Forest Dwellings

desc:
Moving swiftly through the forest, the stream creates a faint veil of sound that
blends with the soft whispering of the surrounding forest. The tall mellyrn
surrounding the stream stretch their branches across the surface of the water,
creating a dazzling display of shadow and light as the light of sun or stars
falls upon the forest of Lothlorien.
name: A Stream through the Forest

desc:
Gathering speed, the small stream runs swiftly and eagerly towards the deep
Celebrant in the southeast. Surrounded by the solemn trees that stand tall and
proud, the babbling brook sings along with the soft rustling of the wind through
the boughs. The scenery, together with the winding forest trail, is splendid,
almost without match.
name: A Forest Stream

desc:
Tall grey mellyrn rise towards the sky all around you, blotting out the light
and shrouding the forest in shadow. No matter which direction you turn, all you
can see is more trees, standing silent and solemn, as if sharing a great secret.
The few breaths of wind that manage to pass here only create faint ripples of
whispers before the secrecy of the forest once again makes the Golden Wood go
silent.
name: In the Heart of Lorien

desc:
Bordered by two straight lines of white stones, the great forest road runs
directly north and south, leading from the fair Celebrant to the marvellous
Caras Galadhon in the north. Standing at a respectful distance, the tall mellyrn
parallel the white stones, seemingly out of reverence for the Elves that dwell
within these woods.
name: Forest Road

desc:
You are in the very heart of the enchanted golden forest of Lothlorien. The
spacious woods stand tall here, reaching towards the heavens, almost as if
yearning for something that is not of this world. Golden and grey, the mellyrn
sway slowly, whispering softly as the wind gently rocks the tree-tops.
name: The Heart of Lorien

desc:
The stream is growing wider and faster as it rushes forth among the great trees
of the golden woods. As it runs along, broad enough now to prevent the
gold-laden boughs of the ancient mellyrn from shadowing its surface, the light
of the stars and the sun sends sparkling displays of light dancing about the
surrounding forest.
name: Forest Stream

desc:
Close to the white stone road, the forest still stands dense, unaffected by the
craftsmanship of the Elves. Tall and proud, the trees rise high towards the sky,
preventing almost all light from reaching the ground. Still the forest is light
and lush and birds thrive among the lower branches.
name: Golden Woods

desc:
Running just outside the great city of Caras Galadhon, this road is paved with
white stones. Laid out in fine patterns, the different shades of white make the
road appear alive, as the light of the city reflects from its surface. Just
outside the circle of the road lie the eaves of the great Golden Wood, the
enchanted forest of Lothlorien, while the inner border of the road passes by a
deep, dark fosse, protecting the enclosed fair city.
name: A White Stone Road

desc:
As the forest opens up to let the land bathe in the light of heaven, the great
paved forest road meets the white stone road that circles the fair city of the
Galadhrim. Just north of the cross lies the graceful bridge that is the entrance
to the capitol of Lothlorien, and branching off to the east into the dense woods
runs a small forest path.
name: End of a Forest Road

desc:
Travelling from west to east, this small forest path follows the deep shadowed
fosse surrounding the high walls of Caras Galadhon for a short distance before
bidding a gentle farewell. Winding carefully among the great mallorn trees, the
path soon vanishes on its continued journey eastwards. To the west, you can
barely discern how the forest opens up, hinting at the passage of a larger road
through the woods that lie so dense south of you. Apart from the whispering of
the forest, you hear an occasional peal of laughter coming from the north, from
within the fair capitol of Lothlorien that stands high upon a green hilltop,
covered in tall trees of unsurpassed greatness and beauty.
name: Forest Path

desc:
Here, at the bottom of the deep shadowed dike that encircles the fair city of
Caras Galadhon, you can only faintly make out any details of the terrain. The
bottom of the fosse is soft and moist as a small stream, emerging from high
above on the slope of the hill, runs along it. The air is moist and filled with
the sounds of the city that filter down from above. The soft splashing of the
slowly flowing water is the only other sound that disturbs the silence. The
stream seems to have an outlet, as the fosse never seems to flood.
name: A Deep Fosse

desc:
Curving softly, the deep fosse lies shrouded in shadow. A soothing splashing
sound can be heard from the small stream flowing onward gently at the bottom of
the elf-made ravine. High above, you can make out the glittering line that
reaches across the fosse, leading from the eaves of the Golden Wood into the
fair city of Caras Galadhon. A low murmur of musical voices filters down upon
you with an occasional burst of merry laughter.
name: A Deep Fosse

desc:
Running in a wide semi-circle, the white stone road that lies just outside the
walls of Caras Galadhon is a work of art as well as master craftsmanship. The
finely chiselled stones are laid out in such a way that they reflect the light
of the city in a myriad different shades of white, creating dazzling displays of
beautiful patterns.
name: A White Stone Road

desc:
The grey trunks of the proud mellyrn rise high towards the sky in this open and
spacious forest. The branches grow wide and stretch far across the open areas,
covering the forest soil in green and golden shadow. As the wind gently blows,
the foliage whispers softly along with the faint song of nightingales.
name: Sparse Woods

desc:
The woods stand quite sparse here, opening up to the skies. The tall grey trees
tower high above you, striving forever upwards. With the forest roads so near,
the underbrush is lessened here, allowing the soil to be illuminated by the
occasional ray of light reaching through the green foilage. Carried from within
the forest, you can hear the song of hidden birds mixed with the song and
laughter of elves living in the woods.
name: Forest by the Road

desc:
The white stones that pave the circular road are laid out to create marvellous
patterns formed by the shifting of nuances, as the lights of the city reflect
and bounce off the stony surface. Bordered by the dense mallorn forest and a
deep shadowed fosse, the white road circles the fair city of Caras Galadhon, the
capital of Lothlorien.
name: A White Stone Road

desc:
The bottom of the deep circular fosse that surrounds the capitol of Lothlorien
is enveloped in soft green shadow. You can feel the moisture in the air rising
from the thin layer of water flowing along the fosse. Just southeast of you, the
great hill rises, covered by the great grey trees of unmatched height and
beauty.
name: A Deep Fosse

desc:
The shadows about you lie deep as you find yourself on the bottom of a deep
fosse. A soft trickle of water runs along the circular base of the great green
hill that is the foundation of the city of Caras Galadhon. Soft voices carry
across the high green wall and the air is filled with laughter and song as yet
another day passes in the enchanted realm of Lothlorien.
name: A Deep Fosse

desc:
The forest ground descends to the north and east in a ragged, brush-choked
slope, and the golden forest thins out during the descent. Through the gaps
between the trees you can see a swift stream on the border, beyond which the
colours of the landscape change to ordinary brown, grey, and dull green. A faint
mist hangs in the air above the stream. Although the eastern bank can be seen
quite distinctly, the land in that direction seems to fade away dizzyingly fast,
as if a gap of many leagues separated it from the borders of the Golden Wood.
name: The Edge of the Golden Wood

desc:
Tall mellyrn surround the road. On its western side the trees grow so thickly,
it is as if they had been planted there to prevent passersby from straying into
the forest. At some distance to the north the road reaches the border of the
Golden Wood. In that direction all colours seem to fade and all shapes seem to
blur, as if you were looking through a piece of uneven glass; it is as though
the air itself had thickened, forming an invisible barrier.
name: A Road through the Golden Wood

desc:
The light is dim and the air itself seems green in the deep shade of tall trees.
Dark green spikes with crowns of lilac-coloured flowers surround the small gully
through which a tiny stream makes its way. No golden trees grow here, and only a
faint memory of their light reaches into this green place - just a distant tinge
of gold, like the receding glow of a sunset when the sun has already set beyond
the treetops.
name: A Dim, Green Gully

desc:
Tall irises stand in ranks on the sides of a small stream that runs through the
forest here, emerging from an unseen opening to the west. Their wavy petals are
deep blue and amethyst violet, and their dark green stems each bear three wide,
flat leaves. The cool fragrance in the air about the flowers seems as refreshing
as the water of the stream that trickles down into the small gully eastwards.
The irises continue halfway into the gully, where they mix with other plants and
finally retreat, as other flowers, lilac-coloured, on taller stems and with
smaller crowns, take over the banks of the stream.
name: Among the Irises

desc:
Turning from the north to the west, the trail crosses a small glade. The grass
in the glade is soft, and the air is almost completely still. The golden forest
around is so quiet that it seems to be sleeping, and its colours seem to be
dimmed. Silence reigns here, and anyone who walks into this glade finds himself
trying to move noiselessly, as if to avoid disturbing the enchanted sleep of the
forest. A few flowers and berries grow here, their stems and leaves clinging to
the ground, and their heads hiding in the grass, as if the plants were also
sleeping.
name: A Trail through a Quiet Glade

desc:
A strange, chilly and serene feeling is in the air around the trail, that turns
from the east to the south as it runs through the forest. Wintry winds do not
reach into the heart of the land of Lorien, yet this part of the forest looks as
if a freezing cold had once touched it, draining it of colour. The crowns of the
mellyrn have paled from golden to almost silver, and the greens of the forest
all seem a hue darker than normal. The drops of water on the leaves and grass
look like tiny crystals of ice, and the silvery cobwebs among the boughs glitter
like frost or miniature icicles. Few sounds can be heard except the ever present
whisper of the wind and the occasional crack of a branch.
name: A Trail through the Quiet Forest

desc:
From the west to the east, the colours of the forest change distinctly, passing
from gold and bright green to pale gold, darker green and silver. The look of
the forest changes together with the colours as if the fabric of the land itself
was sewn together here from two different pieces, of which one is much brighter
in colour than the other, the latter having weathered too many winters. The
boundary seems to pass along a small trail, that runs through the forest just to
the east.
name: Near a Forest Trail

desc:
A light breeze sways the branches of the trees around you. Each time a stronger
gust of wind passes through the forest, a gentle chiming sound comes from the
west. The forest echoes the chiming with tones of its own - pale silvery, deep
golden and clear like the sound of water falling. Then the wind drops, and for a
moment everything is quiet, until, as though aware of the sudden silence, all
the forest birds begin to sing together.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
A few delicate crystal bells hang from a branch of a great mallorn tree. Low
enough to be clearly visible from the ground, yet high enough to be out of reach
even for the tallest of elves, the tiny bells sparkle with the colours of the
rainbow and sway even in the gentlest breeze. The trees around stand still and
quiet, and even the wind itself seems to stop and listen to the gentle chiming.
name: Under the Wind Chimes

desc:
With every breath of the wind, a gentle silvery chiming fills the forest. Coming
from the north, it spreads and multiplies like the sound of an unseen orchestra,
accompanied by the singing of the woodland birds. The gold-laden tree boughs,
their shadows on the ground and the forest flowers seem to sway gently in time
with the music. The forest opens up to the south, where the ground slopes
upwards, approaching the great mound of Cerin Amroth.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
To the south of you rises Cerin Amroth, the tree-covered mound named in memory
of Amroth, the King of Lorien, who dwelt here in the ancient days. As you look
upon it, memories of the days of old come upon you in waves, as distinct as a
sound or a scent and more powerful than your recent memories. It is as though in
the Elder Days the world had been painted with brighter and stronger colours
which are now shining through the pale fabric of this age.
name: At the Foot of Cerin Amroth

desc:
The white trees and the golden trees rise around you, and the South Wind blows
upon Cerin Amroth, sighing among their branches. As you listen, you can hear far
off great seas upon beaches that have long since washed away, and seabirds
crying whose race has vanished from the earth. A ladder hung along the bole of
the tallest mallorn leads to a white flet that gleams amid the foliage.
name: Cerin Amroth

desc:
A wonder comes upon you, as if you have stepped through a high window looking on
a vanished world, on which a light lies for which your language has no name. All
you see is shapely, but the shapes seem at once clear cut, as if they had been
first conceived and drawn at the uncovering of your eyes, and ancient as if they
had endured forever. You see no colour but those you know, gold and white and
blue and green, but they are fresh and poignant, as if you have at that moment
first perceived them and made for them names new and wonderful. In Winter here,
no heart could mourn for Summer or for Spring. No blemish or sickness or
deformity can be seen in anything that grows upon the earth. On the land of
Lorien there is no stain.
name: Cerin Amroth

desc:
Rushing down an eastward slope, a forest stream is interrupted by a low, but
solid rock ledge. Over time, the waters have smoothed a depression in the sandy
soil at the ledge's bottom and, overflowing, continued south. The rock ledge and
tree roots on the banks let the small pool hold shape, as the stream flows
through its centre. Never still, always sparkling and shimmering, the clear
waters reflect the trees, the sky and the white statue of an elven maiden that
stands on the ledge.
name: Sparkling Pool

desc:
Mallorn branches hang so low over the stream it is as if the trees had knelt to
wash them in the water. The water is lucid, and every shadow or line on the
bottom is so clearly visible that the sandy streambed sometimes seems a sunlit
hilly landscape with clouds casting shadows on it as they float by.
name: A Stream in the Golden Wood

desc:
The ground slopes to the east, and the stream turns and gains speed, following
the slope. Mallorn leaves lie on the bottom of the stream and float on its
surface, some of them green and some golden, as if the water had preserved them
unchanged the whole year long. The leaves in the water seem completely untouched
by decay, but some are semi-transparent as if they were not real leaves, but
only reflections that could disappear if somebody stirred the water.
name: A Stream in the Golden Wood

desc:
The mellyrn on this low rise stretch their boughs towards the north, to the
sound and the scent of running water. Closer to the stream, the golden trees are
perched precariously at the very edge of dry land, dipping their long roots into
the current like travellers checking the water to see if it is warm enough to
enter. A small trail meanders among the mellyrn, descends to the stream and
disappears in the water.
name: Forested Rise above a Stream

desc:
Here a stream drops abruptly from the height of about twenty feet into a wide,
bowl-shaped depression and fills it, forming a small lake. Fine white sand
covers the shores of the lake, and many smooth stones of pearl-like colours and
shapes are scattered in the sand. The stones seem to shine from the inside, and
their colours seem to flow and change like the water of the stream. Sometimes
the picture shifts, and for a brief instant the waters of the small lake seem
the waters of the Great Sea splashing on the sand of pearls in Eldamar, the
Elven-home.
name: A Small Lake

desc:
The forest stream bends, passing under the tree branches that almost touch its
surface, and comes splashing down a drop of about twenty feet into a small lake
to the south. Rainbows hang in the fine spray above the small waterfall, where
light breaks on myriad tiny droplets of water. The water is fresh and clear, yet
the air seems to smell of sea salt and the spray seems to have a slightly bitter
taste, like the taste of salt water.
name: Above a Small Waterfall

desc:
Turning from the north to the east, the forest road widens, forming a round
lawn. In the centre of this smooth grassy circle a cluster of blue and white
flowers grows, surrounded by small white stones. A row of larger white stones
marks the lawn's border. Mellyrn surround the grassy circle, but southwards the
ground drops so abruptly that only the high branches of the golden trees reach
the lawn's level.
name: The Circular Lawn

desc:
Rising on the sides of the road are many brightly coloured shrubs, springing out
of the ground like fountains of tiny blossoms. Every shrub is formed by many
thin branches that rise directly from the ground, without a single trunk to
support them. The branches are leafless, covered with tiny blooms that tint the
air around them gold, pink or purple and fill it with a soft fragrance. At a
distance to the northwest, the great hill of Cerin Amroth is visible, and to the
west you catch a glimpse of water sparkling beyond the trees.
name: On the Road through the Flowering Shrubs

desc:
To the west of you stands a great mound, covered with a sward of grass as green
as Spring-time in the Elder Days. Upon it, as a double crown, grow two circles
of trees: the outer have bark of snowy white, and are leafless but beautiful in
their shapely nakedness; the inner are mallorn trees of great height, arrayed in
pale gold. High amid the branches of a towering tree that stands in the centre
of all there gleams a white flet.
name: East of Cerin Amroth

desc:
The great mound that the Elves call Cerin Amroth rises to the west of you. Its
grassy slopes climb up to the circle of white trees which crowns its top.
Although about you living trees and flowers are stirred by the same cool wind
that fans your face, the land around seems a timeless place that does not fade
or change or fall into forgetfulness.
name: East of Cerin Amroth

desc:
The trail runs through the lush undergrowth of the mallorn forest, sometimes
disappearing out of sight beneath the cover of the plants. To the east it
continues deeper into the forest, but to the west it follows a tiny stream that
trickles down a low slope and together with the stream suddenly comes out into
the open. The stream disappears among the mallorn roots, but the trail continues
on its way westward, to where a tree covered mound stands tall beneath a deep
luminous sky.
name: A Trail through the Golden Wood

desc:
Rising over the other trees is a great mallorn, its lofty top blackened and
split in two by a lightning bolt. With the favour and aid of Yavanna, the tree
survived the strike. Its branches still bear clusters of golden flowers in
Spring and green and golden leaves throughout the rest of the year. Yet the
unearthly power of that lightning strike seems to have affected the ancient
tree, imprinting some magic in the wood forever.
name: At the Lightning-struck Tree

desc:
Mallorn branches hang so low over the trail they tug at the hair and clothes of
an unwary traveller. The golden trees themselves seem motionless. Yet with every
movement of air, a windy whisper passes through the grass, rises on the warm air
currents and spreads among the branches, making the leaves tremble slightly as
they pass it on. The trail continues to the east and south.
name: A Trail through the Golden Wood

desc:
A large weathered boulder sits deep in the ground here, crowned by the white and
yellow flowers of a trailing plant. Stumbling upon the boulder, the trail splits
itself in two tracks which run separately for a while, selecting their own paths
among the tree roots until the dips and rises of the forest ground finally bring
them together again. Except for the whisper of the wind in the golden crowns of
the mellyrn, the forest around the trail is as quiet as if it was sleeping as
forests sometimes sleep in the hour before dawn, waiting for the rising sun to
wake all the birds.
name: A Trail through the Golden Wood

desc:
Though the air here is fresh, the colours bright and the shapes clear, for some
reason few birds sing in this part of the forest. There is no wind or movement
of the air and no sound except the sound of water slowly dripping. The golden
trees standing around you seem silent as if they were bound from movement and
sleeping under the spell of a deep sleep from which they could not wake.
name: Forest near a Glen

desc:
A light mist rises from a glen to the south of you, and the golden trees around
seem whitened as if touched by an early frost. Rime seems to cover their
branches and their leaves, and though it is obviously an illusion, it is hard to
shake off the vision and to see the forest again in its bright colours of green
and gold.
name: At a Hazy Glen

desc:
Though standing tall and proud, the golden trees here seem slightly paled and
there is a sense of sadness about them. Like the Elves themselves, this immortal
land cannot be touched by decay; but like the Elves themselves, this land can
fade. All of the colours here are still clear, but dimmed, as fading is neither
a disease nor a distortion. Yet the picture is sorrowful as the passing of such
things may be when the power that keeps them unchanged can hold sway upon them
no longer. Every now and then a momentary change of light makes the paleness
disappear, and the golden branches radiate again in a glory that it would seem
could never pass.
name: Golden Forest

desc:
Golden mellyrn grow on the western side of the road, and silvery birches grow on
its eastern side. As different as the trees are, here they seem to be of almost
the same colour; the white boles of the birches seem to be slightly tinged with
gold, and the mellyrn seem to have paled, acquiring a silvery hue. Northwards
the road approaches the border of Lorien, and although the edge of the Golden
Wood is still miles away, one can already feel the presence of the outside
world, as an alien sound bringing discord in the harmony of the land.
name: A Road through the Golden Wood

desc:
Slender white birches rise all around you, their branches reaching toward the
sky. No mellyrn grow amid the birches, but the air around is permeated by a soft
golden light, as if the radiance of the golden trees was lingering here. In the
mild golden glow the green leaves of the birches seem as fresh and new, as if
they were lit by the Sun of the days when Arda was young. To the west the trees
part, and in the gaps between their silvery boles you can see a grassy lane
running through the forest.
name: Among the Birches

desc:
Narrow paths wind between the tall trees, circling small hills and tussocks
crowned by clusters of blue irises, yellow daffodils, white snapdragons and
lilies of many colours. The scents of the many flowers mingle in the fresh air
together with a light, barely audible sound like the ringing of many tiny bells.
No hedge separates this area from the surrounding forest, yet it is apparent
that this is a garden, tended by caring hands and warmed by the smile of its
fair gardener.
name: Among the Flowers

desc:
The tall golden mellyrn have stepped aside, as if to give more light to the
younger trees that grow here on the slightly elevated forest ground. The young
mellyrn are all only about four feet tall, but their glossy leaves are already
of the same colour and shape, and their slender limbs already have the same
chiselled look as those of the older trees. Behind the trees to the west you can
see a road running from the north to the south through the golden forest. A
faint path leads north.
name: A Cluster of Young Mellyrn

desc:
Standing at a low elevation, the golden trees watch the road that passes through
the forest to the west and south. The warm air beneath the trees is calm like
water in a lake - stirred only by light breezes, it allows you to see far away.
The edge of the golden forest seems to lie just a few miles to the east. In that
direction, ordinary trees are visible through the gold, green and silver of
Lothlorien and are tinted by these colours, as if seen through a painted glass
window.
name: Near the Edge of the Golden Wood

desc:
The road turns from the west to the south, descending from a low elevation. A
faint trail runs away from the road to the east, climbing down a gentle slope.
Looking in that direction, one can see an opening in the boundary of the Golden
Wood where the faint golden glow in the air ebbs and flows, sometimes giving
place to darker, simpler colours of the outer forests. Now and again, the
diffused glow recedes, letting the stark shape of a dark pine come into vision,
and then envelopes it again, giving it a dreamy enchanted look.
name: A Turn in the Road

desc:
Here the road dips, passing through a small depression, and then straightens and
continues at its previous level. Wisps and swirls of warm mist curl above the
grass, indicating that warm underground waters pass here very close to the
surface. Small rainbows twinkle amid the grass, stretching from the tip of one
blade of grass to the tip of another as if supported by invisible gossamer
threads.
name: A Road through the Golden Wood

desc:
The ground here slopes downward from the west to the east, and the road has to
bend slightly to stay at one level. The golden trees at the western side of the
road are sparse, and the ground below them is covered with a carpet of flowers.
Small rose-like flowers, blue and purple, run out onto the road down the western
slope and stop a few feet from the road's edge.
name: A Road through the Golden Wood

desc:
The road runs from the north to the south through the golden forest. At a
distance to the south you can see the green hill of Caras Galadhon. The elven
city is still too far away to see clearly, yet it is impossible not to feel its
presence. Every gaze and every movement, the course of every road, trail or
stream in the land, every current of air seems to be directed to the distant
city, bound by the power and light that flow from it.
name: A Road through the Golden Wood

desc:
The golden trees stand slightly apart here, and in the gaps between their boles
to the east and to the south you can see a wide curve of a road passing through
the forest. The road makes a turn from the north to the west as it approaches a
white staircase that leads up to a green wall. The white stairs glitter from
afar as though covered by snow or ice, yet the glitter does not seem to be cold
and remote like that of ice on a mountain peak - rather, it might remind one of
the whiteness of a snowy hill atop which the lights of a village merrily
twinkle.
name: The Edge of the Golden Wood

desc:
Here, beneath the eaves of the Golden Wood, a light lies on tree and shrub, on
leaf and flower, on the grass and amid the branches. Clear and golden, it seems
to spring not from a source in the visible world, but from the depths of time,
like the light which seems to shine from the darkened pages of ancient books or
from the fair elven faces unmarred by age and untouched by darkness.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
The air beneath the eaves of the golden forest is full of a sweet mingled scent
that seems to bring forth memories, making them as distinct as scents or sounds.
As they blend with the picture, even time itself begins to seem an illusion, an
invention by the clock-makers for making the clocks run. Gaps of many centuries
seem no greater than an hour, and deeds of a thousand years ago seem as alive as
if they had happened today before breakfast. The silvery trunks, the singing of
birds, the changing of light and shadows all seem to be part of one eternal
picture, as old as the sun and the stars.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
Turning from the west to the south, the road passes by a great chestnut tree.
Coloured in earthy brown and deep green hues, the tree is sharply silhouetted
against the golden, bright green and silvery grey backdrop of the mellyrn.
Whether its stature and girth are due to its own life force or to the soil and
the air of the Golden Wood, the tree seems quite determined to outgrow its
golden neighbours. Chestnuts are scattered on the ground all around its roots,
most of them broken or gnawed by the teeth of squirrels or other omnipresent
forest creatures. A few saplings of the same earthy colours grow around it,
their leaves smaller replicas of the leaves of the great chestnut.
name: At a Bend in the Forest Road

desc:
Changing its direction ever so slightly to avoid the rising ground, the road
passes between two slopes. The northern slope is covered with mellyrn that climb
high over the golden forest to watch the road from above. The southern slope is
covered mostly by dark green ferns. Brightly coloured sparks glimmer here and
there among the ferns, vanishing whenever one tries to focus one's eyes on them
and instantly reappearing elsewhere on the slope. Ahead to the west, the road
skirts a limestone outcropping and turns, disappearing from sight; eastwards it
continues straight on its way through the golden forest.
name: A Road through the Golden Wood

desc:
Turning from the east to the north, the forest road skirts a limestone
outcropping, leaving it to the southwest. About two yards high, the outcropping
is composed of a dozen layers of limestone, interspersed with a few darker
stripes. The warm, even weather of the Golden Wood has allowed the greyish slab
to preserve its original shape, yet the forest plants have finally begun to take
their toll on it. The winds have carried some soil and seeds onto the surface of
the slab and into the cracks on it, allowing many grasses and flowers to take
root. Blue sage grows supported in the cracks at the base of the outcropping,
and a cluster of bright stonecrops crowns its top. From beyond the outcropping,
to the southwest, the singing of water is heard at a distance, where a stream
seems to drop down a small waterfall.
name: On a Road Passing a Limestone Outcropping

desc:
The road runs from the north to the south here, on its journey from the mound of
Cerin Amroth to the elven city of Caras Galadhon. To the south, it approaches a
limestone outcropping, and northwards it plunges deeper into the forest. Through
some trick of light or an illusion, the grassy surface of the road to the north
looks so smooth that it appears to shine, reflecting light. From beyond the
trees to the west you can hear the merry babble of a forest stream, which seems
to be talking too fast for you to make out any words.
name: A Road through the Golden Wood

desc:
The road turns from the south to the west where it widens, forming a circular
lawn. Rowan trees cluster to the north and east of the road, silhouetted sharply
against the backdrop of mellyrn. Their branches extend over the road, casting
striped shadows across the grassy surface. Clusters of bright red and orange
rowan berries hang from the branches, a delicious fare for many birds if Winter
were ever to come to this land.
name: On a Road through the Rowan Trees

desc:
A few darker and thinner boles stand in contrast amidst the silvery boles of the
mellyrn, and tracing their trunks upwards with your eyes you can see that they
belong to rowans. Clusters of red berries hang from the branches of the rowans,
bright yet barely noticeable amid the splendour of the golden trees.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
The clearing is completely overgrown with flowers. To the edge of the clearing
the flowers grow in shades of white, yellow and pink. Flowers in shades of red
dominate in the centre of the clearing, where a small mound is surrounded by red
and orange marigolds and crowned by a bush of dark red roses. Like a golden
frame encircling a jewelled ornament, golden mellyrn stand around the clearing.
Their leaves, when touched by a light wind, seem to ring gently.
name: A Clearing in the Golden Wood

desc:
Some warm Lorien years ago either a few saplings were planted in this clearing
between the tall mellyrn, or a few cherry pits and apple seeds were dropped
carelessly on the ground after a picnic. Fed by the fertile soil and the soft
rains that fell on the forest day after day, the seeds or saplings have grown
into large trees. Their spreading branches bear gentle pink and white blossoms
in the Spring, bright green leaves in the Summer, large yellow apples or
clusters of red cherries in the Autumn, and then stand leafless through the
Winter watching the Sun and the Moon rise and set, and the stars roll by in the
Winter sky. Elves and forest creatures come here now and then, to pay their
respects to the trees and their sweet fruit.
name: Among Apple and Cherry Trees

desc:
Winding among the boles of the trees, the trail runs west and north as it passes
a large mallorn. The branches of the golden tree bend almost to the ground, and
a glistening rope ladder climbs up the bole, blending with the silvery bark. The
grass just below the tree is sprinkled with white and yellow flowers, growing in
clusters near the base of the tree, as though somebody had spilled them there
while climbing the ladder.
name: A Trail through the Golden Wood

desc:
Lying just off a small trail that passes through the forest to the west, this
glade is almost entirely covered in mushrooms. Mushrooms grow in the grass, in
the moss by the tree trunks and on the moss-covered stones that sit on the
ground. Only the trees that stand around the glade have escaped their attention,
as they rise over the host of brightly coloured caps like fairy tale giants
watching a crowd of little people. The mushrooms are grey, brown, yellow, orange
and red; some clearly edible, others less tempting, but most of them are
unfamiliar kinds. Here and there, broken caps and stems lie on the ground where
some woodland creatures feasted on their favourite fare.
name: A Glade near a Trail

desc:
To the east of you the forest ground slopes down into a small glen, the sides of
which are covered by soft green moss and little white flowers. A faint mist
rolls slowly out of the glen, and the golden forest beyond its veil looks pale,
unreal and a little sad, like a landscape seen in a dream just before you are
awakened by the bright sunlight.
name: At a Hazy Glen

desc:
Green moss covers the bottom of the glen, and a faint mist rises above it, white
and chilly near the ground but almost invisible in the air. Behind the thin
curtain of the mist, the golden trees look drawn as if with age, and pale as if
an early winter was upon them.
name: In a Hazy Glen

desc:
Twilight lingers in the secret glade, hidden and protected from strangers' eyes
by a circle of dark elms and pale hemlock-umbels. A grey rope ladder leads up
the bole of a tall elm into the canopy of tree branches. Silvery moths flutter
above the soft grass, and a faint enchanting tune hangs in the air, as if an
unseen piper was playing a flute, waiting for a dark-haired elf to step into the
circle of trees and start a dance.
name: Hidden Glade

desc:
A grass-covered lane leads through the golden forest, looking more like a
recently mowed lawn than like a forest road. Golden mellyrn rise to both sides
of the lane, making no attempts to step on its grassy surface, as if they had
willingly moved aside to let the travellers pass.
name: A Road through the Golden Wood

desc:
No wheel-tracks or hoofprints mar the springy carpet of grass which covers the
road, softening every footfall. Little flowers lift their heads above the grass,
as though they were curious about something and not afraid of being stepped on.
Tall mellyrn rise on both sides of the road, their boles and branches slightly
tilted, as if the trees were watching the passersby.
name: A Road through the Golden Wood

desc:
The road runs steadily from the south to the north on its way from the city of
Caras Galadhon to the outer reaches of the forest of Lothlorien. Golden trees
rise around the road, and their leaves seem to be whispering and ringing at
once, as if trying both to talk and to laugh. Now and then, the sounds stop for
a moment and then renew, as if the forest was watching you with curiosity and
barely concealed mirth.
name: A Road through the Golden Wood

desc:
The road turns from the north to the east, passing a low hill to the west. Winds
blow from the hill onto the road incessantly, making the grassy surface swirl
and ripple. When the waves travelling across the road stop for a moment, the
grasses straighten their blades and the pink anemones that grow by the western
edge of the road rearrange their petals, only to be ruffled and brought into
disarray again by the next playful breeze.
name: A Road through the Golden Wood

desc:
A sparkling spring bursts out of the grass here and trickles back into the
ground between the mallorn roots. Warmed by the rays of the sun and fed by the
waters of the spring, flowers grow here as fair as any flowers on this side of
the Sea, bright with the colours of dawn and the sky, their fragrance like a
memory of dewy mornings under the unshadowed sun in the Spring of Arda.
name: Flowering Slope

desc:
Here low bushes and shrubs have claimed most of the ground between the silvery
boles of the mellyrn. The undergrowth seems to be confused by the warm weather,
as some of the bushes are sprouting new leaves while some are flowering and the
branches of the others are bearing fruit. A few flat stones, each of them about
one foot long, have been set in the ground at regular intervals, as if marking a
path to tread upon. Some of the stones appear to have sunk deep into the soft
turf, leaving gaps much wider than an elf could cross in one step. The path
turns from the east to the west, where it turns into what appears to be a
courtyard running through the forest.
name: On a Small Path

desc:
The golden mellyrn rise proudly around you, their boles like chiselled grey
pillars and their crowns like a sea of living gold beside which any gold of the
dwarves would seem dull and bland, just a lifeless imitation of the glorious
golden trees. Here under the eaves of this forest the air itself seems golden;
here no evil can survive in the golden glow.
name: The Heart of the Golden Wood

desc:
The colours here are breathtakingly bright and clear, and the air is as fresh
and fragrant as if a wind had just brought it here from the gardens and the
snow-capped mountains of Valinor. Timeless, young and ancient, the immortal
golden forest stands like a palace crafted from live trees on one of the first
days of creation, like a memorial to the First Age and to Arda the unmarred.
name: The Heart of the Golden Wood

desc:
The mallorn trees stand slightly apart here, reaching for each other with their
long branches. The air is fresh and clear, and every sound echoes and spreads as
if you were not in a forest but in a great hall with a roof of gold and pillars
of silver. Above the roof of the golden crowns there seems to be yet another
roof, the faraway sky, which here does not seem so distant and looks as if one
could almost reach it from a golden treetop.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
This hill is formed by two slopes that join at an angle, climb steeply to the
highest point and then run slowly down into the golden forest. A grassy road
skirts the foot of the hill, turning from the north to the east. Young mallorn
trees climb up the green slopes to the golden cluster of trees that crowns the
crest of the hill; the grass between their boles is starred with white and
yellow flowers.
name: On a Forested Hill above the Road

desc:
The golden forest is alive with the song of nightingales in the tree branches.
When the birds stop for a moment catching their breaths, the distant sound of
flowing water can be heard, merging with the whisper of the leaves carried in
the wind. It is impossible to determine from which direction it comes, and
sometimes it seems to come from more than one source at once, as if many hidden
streams were joining here on their way to Celebrant, to follow it on its journey
to Anduin and to the distant Sea.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
Barely visible in tall grass, a faint path or trail comes from the east and ends
at a low grassy mound crowned with a cluster of flowers, each one the size of a
small rose, with purple petals surrounding a white core. More flowers of this
kind grow at the base of the mallorn trees, looking even more shapely next to
the great silvery boles. The golden radiance that permeates the air of the
mallorn wood seems to cling to the purple flowers, outlining every delicate
petal.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
This clearing looks like one of the places where Lorien elves plant the flower
seeds they bring to Lorien from the other lands. White, yellow and blue flowers
cover the ground here so thickly, that there seems to be no spot where you could
set your foot without stepping on a flower. Garden flowers, forest flowers and
field flowers all grow together, forgetting their preferences and prejudices
about surroundings, landscape, soil and lighting. To the west the clearing
continues, and flowers in shades of red seem to replace flowers in shades of
blue.
name: A Clearing in the Golden Wood

desc:
Large, brightly coloured flowers cover the soft ground under the great mallorn.
A low hedge with an ivy-covered gate in it encloses the flower patch and the
tree. Here and there beehives and honeycombs can be seen among the branches.
Countless bees fly all around the golden tree, as if it was one giant beehive.
The low buzzing of the bees mingles with a clear sound of flowing water. The air
is scented with flowers and honey.
name: Under the Great Mallorn

desc:
Bright white light streams down from the crown of a mallorn tree, casting
wavering shadows on the grass. A thick cloud of mist shrouds the tree branches,
completely concealing the source of the glow. The gold and the green of the
mellyrn are pale from the mist and strangely bright light. The grass beneath the
golden trees is in places quite short, as if it has grown just recently;
although the flowers have fully opened blooms, they are also of a short stature,
as if something had stopped or slowed their growth.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
The golden trees stand slightly apart here, and the forest soil beneath them is
covered not with grass, but with a carpet of thick green moss. To the north of
you the ground slopes down into a deep glen. A barely visible mist or a haze
hangs over the glen, a barely discernible haze that does not obscure the view,
yet gives the picture a slight semblance of unreality as if the whole place
could be gone if you only blinked your eyes.
name: At a Hazy Glen

desc:
Many flowers and tiny shrubs cluster around the hollow tree that grows from the
ground here, leafless but seemingly quite alive. Whatever accident or whimsy of
nature had created it, it apparently failed to destroy the tree's living energy
and spirit. Thin vines with tiny white flowers climb its sides, forming a wreath
on its top. Assembled around this strange, flower-crowned apparition, the plants
look like a crowd of woodland creatures performing some rite of Spring.
name: At a Hollow Tree

desc:
To the east, behind a line of mellyrn, a road passes through the wood, its
surface smooth and glittering from a distance like a river of green. In all the
other directions the forest of Lorien spreads in a dream-like golden haze where
all shapes and lines seem to blend together, yet grow distinct and sharp when
you let your gaze rest upon them.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
Among the golden trees on this hill the West Wind itself seems to have taken its
abode, singing with the nightingales, tugging at the branches, flapping the
leaves, and playing with light and shadows. Here the wind brings scents of
strange flowers, grasses and trees from distant lands, and weaves them into the
gentle breezes of the Golden Wood. Here it sometimes brings also strains of
sweet music, fairer than any flower scent yet shorter than the fall of a drop of
rain.
name: On a Windy Hill

desc:
Lying in the midst of the golden forest, this courtyard passes between two lines
of slender trees of white bark that stand on its sides like colonnades. It runs
from the east to the west to what appears to be a clearing in a circle of
mellyrn. The tessellated floor is half white marble, half the living forest
floor covered with thick green grass, in places decorated with tiny bluebells or
tiny flowers of elanor. This mosaic of marble and grass runs to the very
entrance of the mallorn circle where it stops at a grassy threshold.
name: Tree-surrounded Courtyard

desc:
Mallorn trees surround this woodland temple on all sides, interspersed with
slender columns that branch into airy arches at about three yards above the
ground. Between the arches and the branches, many panes of matte and transparent
glass are suspended. Bright images are painted or stained upon some panes, while
others are blank as though left as a background for one perfect mallorn branch
silhouetted against them. The ground is paved with white marble except in the
very centre of the circle, where a large altar-like stone sits in the soft
earth. Cool water emerges from an opening on its top and trickles down the side,
and the ground around it is covered with pale flowers of niphredil. A low
railing surrounds the circle, the only opening in it allowing entrance from the
east. But to the west, the mallorn trees themselves seem to have stepped aside
where an arch opens above the railing like a window, letting the westering sun
enter here as it dips below the tops of the golden trees on its way across the
Sea, to Elvenhome and to the Blessed Lands beyond.
name: Woodland Shrine

desc:
The small oval glade is covered by a carpet of ankle-deep green grass, out of
which a few long-stemmed flowers lift their heads. Dug into the ground in the
very centre of the glade is a basin of white stone sprinkled with silvery
specks. The basin is about six feet wide, and its rim is carved in the shape of
a wreath of mallorn leaves. Clear water fills the basin, and its surface
reflects the golden trees around the glade, their crowns climbing tier upon tier
like clusters of sunset clouds, or like golden towers of a city built upon a
mountain.
name: A Glade in the Golden Wood

desc:
Two barely visible trails, marked only by a few bent plant stems, meet beneath
an oval opening in the canopy formed by the mallorn branches. A hint of music
hangs in the air here, a tune on the very edge of hearing that seems to fade
whenever you strain your ears trying to make it out.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
In a secluded clearing surrounded closely by the golden trees, a small,
grass-covered mound rises from the ground. It is likely of elven construction,
as its shape appears too regular and its sides too smooth to be natural. White
flowers crowning its top suggest that this is a burial mound, although there is
nothing else about it or its surroundings that is mournful or reminiscent of
death and the passage of time. Bright flowers grow all around it and climb
halfway up its sides, and birds sing cheerfully in the treetops around the
clearing.
name: At a Small Mound

desc:
As if fingers of transparent mist were floating in the air, or a hidden fire was
burning somewhere, making warm draughts rise from the ground, the images of the
trees around you sometimes waver and become blurred before coming into focus
again. Even those mellyrn that appear unobscured by any haze or illusion seem
bleaker and paler than the golden trees just to the south. Below the trees, the
grass is silvery as if covered with dew or raindrops and sprinkled with many
tiny white flowers that give off a soft fragrance.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
In the everchanging pattern of light and shadow that lies on the forest floor
here there is one square-shaped shadow that does not change as the branches of
the trees move. Barely visible in the foliage of the mallorn tree above the
shadowy square is a grey platform, to which a silvery ladder leads along the
bole of the tree.
name: Golden Wood

desc:
This light platform was built on a junction of two sturdy mallorn branches and
has no walls or railing. A grey rope ladder leads down through a round opening
in the middle of the platform. Built with care and without excessive decoration,
the flet seems to be as much a part of the tree as the leaves and the branches.
With the exception of an old chest and a low bench there is no furniture, but it
seems to be just enough, as if anything else would have broken the harmony of
the picture.
name: On a Flet

desc:
Two grey ropes form this slender bridge that leads through the tree branches,
from one flet to another. The ropes pass side by side, sometimes resting on
mallorn branches or by the side of a tree trunk, sometimes bound together by
bands of grey cloth or a stem of a climbing plant. Although the bridge might
seem flimsy, it is apparently quite stable - the ropes don't bend under your
weight and don't slide when you move, as if some invisible force was keeping
them in place.
name: On a Rope Bridge

desc:
Suspended on silvery ropes descending from the crowns of the tall mellyrn, this
