flet seems to have no support from below or on either side. Every breath of wind
through the grey boughs sends minute tremors through the floor, yet the flet
does not shift even slightly, as if held in place by an unseen power. A low
round table in the middle of the flet holds a crystal bowl with water collected
in it. A grey silken hammock in the branches above appears to serve as the
sleeping place for the owner of this dwelling; blue fringes of the covers hang
over its side.
name: On a Small Flet

desc:
A faint, greenish light filters through the roof of grass, turf and branches
into this dark, warm grotto. Piles of leaves and twigs, both fresh and dry,
cover the ground, rustling and crackling with every movement in the cavern. In
the faint greenish radiance coming from above even the dry leaves on the floor
seem green, and the greyish moss on the walls seems more alive than it would
otherwise look. Thin vines with tiny pale flowers on them entwine with the
branches on the ground and climb up the mossy walls to the roof.
name: A Dim, Green Cavern

desc:
The walls, floor and ceiling of this hollow are carpeted completely by soft,
green moss. Here and there, water-drops and spider webs glisten faintly on the
green cover. Across the cavern floor, tracks of some large animal lead to the
northern wall, where a few pinecones and branches lie half-submerged in the
moss. The air is almost still, but fresh, being scented by pine-needles and wet
earth, perhaps washed by an underground spring.
name: Mossy Cavern

desc:
Beehives of all sizes and honeycombs of all shapes cover the spreading branches
of the golden tree. A low buzzing, louder humming and an angry drone fill the
air, drowning out all the other sounds of the forest. The strong, sweet scent of
honey is overpowering; a deep breath could be enough to make one dizzy from the
sweetness.
name: In the Branches of the Great Mallorn

desc:
Cradled in the branches of a great mallorn tree, this wooden platform is so
light that it seems the tree is holding it on the tips of its branches. A low
railing runs along its edges and a woven screen stands to one side. Light of sun
and stars, warm winds, scents of earth, grass and flowers, and singing of birds
are all free to enter this airy dwelling with no roof but for the forest canopy.
Here, one could live for ages, forgetting about the ground, using the
surrounding branches for support and breathing the sweet forest air.
name: On a Light Platform

desc:
High above the shimmering golden forest, a lofty flet lies nestled in the
branches of a tall mallorn. A light seems to lie on the land around you, shining
through every leaf and flower; a power seems to be in every tree, spring or
stone, and a memory of ages long gone and forgotten seems to flow through the
air.
name: On the White Flet

desc:
Bundles of ash and other wood are stacked high against one wall whereas the rest
of the structure boasts the tools of a skilled craftsman. Large windows and an
arched doorway offer a glimpse into the golden forest outside. Sounds of a
bubbling brook enter through the western window and the gentle fragrance of
flowers permeates the air. A small forge sits in one corner, whereas the meagre
living quarters are barely concealed by a curtain just opposite.
name: Thatched Workshop

desc:
As the cavern extends from the south to the north and east, widening slightly,
the soft forest soil of the floor and the ceiling is replaced by limestone. Over
time, tree roots crumbled the formerly solid rock, first causing fissures to
appear on it, then causing first smaller crumbs and then larger chunks to detach
and fall off. As the soil mixed with limestone crumbs, moss overtook the cavern
floor allowing vines to clamber up the walls, taking hold in every crack. The
sound of water can be heard from the east, mixing with its own echoes to create
an almost uniform din.
name: Cavern under the Forest

desc:
Damp darkness reigns in this underground hollow. In the gloom, many whispers
travel from one unseen wall to another, tangling among the overhanging roots. No
words or pattern can be distinguished in these whispers, and it seems as if the
trees were talking to each other and to the earth itself. Far away, faint yet
distinct, a different sound can be heard - the sound of running water.
name: Cavern amid the Roots

desc:
Year after year, water dripping from the ceiling of the cavern dissolved the
limestone, allowing it to form icicle-like stalactites on the ceiling and
stalagmites below them on the floor. Some of these have grown towards each other
until they joined, forming uneven pillars. The northern wall of the cavern
appears to be solid, but the southern wall has an oval opening in it with a
steady cascade of water dropping behind it, filling the air with fine spray and
a fresh scent. Echoing among the stalactites and stalagmites in the cavern, the
sound of flowing water is multiplied until it turns into a roaring din as if the
Sea itself was beating at the shore just outside the walls of this cavern.
name: Among the Stalactites

desc:
This natural cave is airy and spacious. Walls of light stone rise toward a
cupola-shaped ceiling that recedes into darkness. Patches of grass billow from a
few cracks in the walls and the ground, vigorous despite the everlasting
twilight. The floor of the cavern is equally split between soil and stone. The
soil patches carry distinct marks of large paws.
name: A Natural Cavern

desc:
The floor of this cramped chamber is lined with wooden beams, their surface
dusty and spotted. Dead golden leaves glimmer along their edges. The walls
narrow quickly toward a square spot at the very centre of the domed ceiling. A
rough but recognisable white outline of a hand, its finger pointed upwards, palm
forward, marks the north wall.
name: Narrow Shaft

desc:
A foot-high square grey monolith, four hand-spans wide, crowns the hill. The
summit of the hill bears a scattering of young hesitant mellyrn and this stone,
enigmatically placed in the middle. A majestic view opens from here on the
forest, the mountains, and the rivers surrounding and passing through the land.
The hill descends sharply in all directions, but some stairs lead from the
summit to a level clearing below the top. The southern side of the hill is
nearly vertical and bare, while the others have some vegetation and undersized
mellyrn struggling to hang on to them.
name: Finwe's Stone

desc:
Vaulting high up the eastern slope of the heavily forested hill, this staircase
makes its way up the final stretch of the path toward the summit, seemingly
struggling to hold on to the sharp grade, much like the proud mallorn trees
around it. Stone slabs, slightly caved-in from wear, comprise most of it, except
for a few steps at the bottom where the ground is held together with dead birch
branches. A fantastic view of the forest predominantly of pale green and gold
opens from here, though a traveller would do well to be cautious should he
choose to enjoy it - the staircase is steep and there is no handhold.
name: Rocky Staircase

desc:
This enormous beehive with a narrow opening to the north is a tempest of sound
and fury. Bees, bees, and more bees dart back and forth in some order that only
they can perceive. A modicum of fresh air makes it in through the tiny hexagonal
ventilated openings along the top. Such openings are spread all down the sides
of the beehive, their purpose there no longer to pass air but to hold that which
is the most precious product of honeybees' daily labour.
name: Within a Giant Beehive

desc:
A multitude of glorious golden beehives stand in rows here, constructed and
maintained with care and affection by the elves of the Golden Wood, for they are
the source of their favourite drink. The pyramidal shapes glitter in the shade
of this small forest nook, concealed from outside by trees, moss and old leaves.
Merry buzzing permeates the air, getting louder to the south.
name: Among the Beehives

desc:
This beautifully crafted bridge stretches across the deep, shadowed fosse in a
graceful arch. Although it is broad enough to carry several elves, there are no
pillars supporting its weight, which gives to it a sense of frailty. The low
railings are meticulously carved, inlaid with silver and gold. Several arcane
symbols can be seen on the railings, as well as rare gems forming mysterious
patterns.
name: Arched Bridge

desc:
In the deep lane formed between the green walls, the Great Gates of Caras
Galadhon are set. The path is laid with stones, placed in such a way as to
create vivid patterns on the ground. The sides of the walls are supported by
carved and treated wood, with the symbols of Celeborn and Galadriel inlaid in
silver.
name: The Gates of Caras Galadhon

desc:
You find yourself on a small path that climbs the gently sloping hill. Laid with
white stones in wonderful patterns, the trail carefully avoids the golden trees
standing on the hill. Just south of you are the great gates of Caras Galadhon,
and to your west the green towering wall rises sharply.
name: Path by the Gates

desc:
A small semicircle of marble benches stands at the foot of a large mallorn. In
the centre of the circle there is a table, a chair and a couple of weapon racks.
Lying on the table are lists of guards and schedules. The racks are full of
weapons for use by the elves on guard and by other residents of Caras Galadhon
should the city be under siege. A ladder stands against the wall that towers
south of here, providing easy access to the top.
name: Peleryn of the Guards

desc:
Small paths form large intricate patterns amidst the lush bright flowers that
grow here. The few mellyrn standing around almost seem to have deliberately left
this space open to let the sun and stars shine unhindered upon the dazzling
display of elanor and niphredil.
name: Galadriel's Garden

desc:
The small glittering stream gains speed here, running swiftly southward in eager
anticipation towards its union with the greater waters of the world. Towering
above is the great wall surrounding the city, and all around you is the garden
of Galadriel - enchanted, beautiful and immortal.
name: Fountain Stream

desc:
The river runs down into the ground below the wall, babbling and sparkling as
the last rays of light gradually filter away. Turning slightly, the stream
hurries onward, never hesitating, into the deeper shadows.
name: Underground Stream

desc:
Completely submerged in water, you barely manage to see that the dark is being
replaced by grey shadows as the water pushes on. The walls have been polished
smooth by the flowing water. There is nowhere you can get a firm grip or
foothold as the stream drags you along farther southward.
name: Underground Stream

desc:
Skirting the great wall, the trail darts in and out of the woods, twisting and
turning. Both to the north and to the east the path vanishes quickly among the
mighty golden trees that stand towering above you. From within the reaches of
the enchanted forest you can hear the faint whispers of the trees and the soft
singing of the ever-present nightingales.
name: Path below the Wall

desc:
The small path runs up the hill, winding and twisting to avoid the trees that
surround it. A few steps of marble here and there ease the ascent where the
slope of the hill is more steep. From the south voices can be heard, carried on
a light breeze from the great gates of the city that can just barely be
discerned among the golden trees that stand high and proud all around you. The
path beneath your feet runs both west and south from here.
name: Winding Path up the Hill

desc:
You find yourself on the southern slope of the hill among the large mellyrn that
are the heart of the golden forest. The forest opens up slightly to your north
higher on the slope where the elves have planted a beautiful vineyard. Just east
of here you can spot a thick hedge, preventing passage both east and south,
although there seems to be a small passage in it that could lead you onward.
name: Golden Forest

desc:
The forest opens up, allowing the light of heavens to fall down onto Galadriel's
enchanted garden. Solemn and lonely, a few mellyrn stand towering in the midst
of a mosaic of colours. Narrow, well-trodden paths form strange patterns amongst
the flowers, separating them into fields of dazzling and sparkling beauty. The
chirping of nightingales can be heard from all around against the quiet babbling
of the fountain stream.
name: Galadriel's Garden

desc:
A narrow, gracefully arched bridge leads across the sparkling fountain stream
connecting the garden of Galadriel with the valley that is home to the Mirror.
The bridge is cut out of white stone, polished and inlaid with silver strands,
making it shine and sparkle in the lights of the city. During cloudless dark
nights the light of the stars is reflected by the surface of the bridge,
throwing cascades of light onto the flowing water below it.
name: A Graceful Bridge

desc:
A small valley lies hidden here within dense hedges, yet open to the skies
above. Not much disturbs the peace and silence, almost as if the living forest
around was showing respect towards this obscured place. In the centre of the low
green hollow stands a single white basin, sparkling as light reflects from its
surface.
name: At the Mirror of Galadriel

desc:
Among the towering mellyrn of the golden forest the wind whispers softly,
carrying words of power and wisdom. Throughout the woods soft voices can be
heard. A small stair of white polished marble ascends the hillside, beckoning
travellers to climb and get a better view, but the trees stand too thickly to
allow much to be seen. The path quickly hides behind the trees both to the north
and to the south, as the golden forest stretches out to cover and shelter its
inhabitants.
name: The Winding Path

desc:
Just off the path into the magnificent forest, a few bells hang from a branch.
As the wind blows gently, the bells create quiet and beautiful sounds and
harmonies at the very edge of your hearing. Close to your east you can spot a
small vineyard that lies in a clearing. North, up the slope of the hill, the
trees stand denser but still the light of skies manages to filter down through
the canopy, dancing across the leaves and undergrowth.
name: Below the Bronze Bells

desc:
This vineyard grows on the bank of the fountain stream. Here the elves grow the
large grapes from which they make the delicious wines which are served with
their meals. The golden forest itself has graciously stepped aside, allowing the
vines ample space to grow strong and healthy.
name: A Beautiful Vineyard

desc:
As the stream leaves the crown of the hill, it starts its long journey towards
the great rivers. Hurrying down the slope, the water almost seems alive,
jumping, laughing and teasing as it travels through the softly whispering woods.
name: Fountain Stream

desc:
Twisting and turning between the trees, the stream is making its way down the
slope slowly and gently. Always sparkling and babbling, the water fills the
surrounding woods with a soft soothing veil of sound. Flowing south, the small
fountain stream rushes below a bridge of white stone before continuing its long
journey towards its distant goal.
name: Fountain Stream

desc:
A large mallorn stands here, its branches reaching high up into the sky. Soft
voices can be heard from a large flet that rests among the branches of the tree.
A ladder stands against the bole of the mallorn, leading up towards the flet. As
the trail continues east, the dense trees open up into a small clearing from
which you can hear soft music being played.
name: Peleryn of the Bards

desc:
Here the bards of Caras Galadhon hold their assembly and this is where they sing
and play, performing and telling tales. Anyone who wants to is welcome to listen
and share their tales. Even when the clearing is empty, the surrounding trees
seem to sing in soft whispers the tales of the bards. In the centre of this
small clearing stands a polished marble statue in the shape of a harp. As the
soft winds rustle through the clearing, the strings of the harp are set in
motion, creating quiet, gentle music.
name: A Vibrant Clearing

desc:
Close to the high wall surrounding the city, the ground levels out until the
slope is almost insignificant. The dense forest approvingly gives room to the
wall that rises sharply in the east. Just to the north, the forest opens up
around a large, ancient mallorn standing in solemn and quiet contemplation. To
the west you can barely discern a path running through the woods.
name: Golden Forest

desc:
One of the tallest trees in Caras Galadhon towers towards the sky here. Not only
does it dwarf the surrounding mellyrn, but it rises high above the wall as well.
All around the tree the forest is dense, but closer to the wall the woods are
more sparse, almost as if the trees were keeping the distance out of respect.
name: At a Large Mallorn

desc:
You find yourself on the eastern slope of Caras Galadhon close to the great
defensive wall of the city. The trees do not grow too close to the wall, so the
forest is rather open and light. The winds play freely among the branches, and
the leaves rustle softly above you.
name: Golden Forest

desc:
Bending slowly, the twisting trail runs up the slope, climbing higher and higher
towards the crown of the hill. Passing between the tall mellyrn, the trail
evades them carefully, as if it does not want to disturb the ancient trees. Just
west off the path one can spot some trees bearing large yellow fruits, but the
path turns away from them, continuing south and east.
name: Bend in the Winding Path

desc:
Stretching along the slope of the hill, the path can follow a nearly straight
course here, diverting only infrequently to pass by the trees that grow less
densely in this part of the forest. Just east of here it forks, travelling both
east and south. To the west the path also curves softly southwards. Although
rooted in ground lower down the slope to the north, a tall mallorn towers high
above you, dwarfing the surrounding trees.
name: The Winding Path

desc:
As the path reaches the crown of the hill, the golden forest opens up
graciously, letting the trail split into a fork. Reaching into the heart of
Caras Galadhon, the path travels towards the shimmering white fountain lying
just south of here. Northward it travels down the slopes into the surrounding
forest, passing among the trees crowded with the flets of the Galadhrim.
name: A Fork in the Path

desc:
Running along the slope of the hill, the small trail leads through the golden
forest, twisting and turning to avoid the large trees. Tall and mighty, the
surrounding mellyrn rise above the path, hiding it from the sky and shrouding it
in soft shadows. The soft whispers of the trees mingle with the singing of birds
to create a feeling of tranquility as well as serenity.
name: A Path through the Golden Forest

desc:
Bending sharply, the path continues through the forest, running along the gentle
slope. The trees are not very dense here, letting the light of the skies fall
directly on the ground, creating pictures of gold, green and black in the
undergrowth. Just east of the trail is a dense stand of young trees striving for
space in youthful competition, and north of here the woods open up into a large
clearing. Continuing south and west, the trail vanishes among the trees as it
curves around the hill.
name: Curving Path

desc:
What must have been a clearing just a few years ago is now full of young trees,
striving towards the sky. Reaching only a few yards up in the air, the young
mellyrn are unhindered in their growth as they are widely spaced and cared for
by the elves.
name: A Stand of Young Mellyrn

desc:
Following the slope, the trail curves gently as it runs west and south. As the
tall trees stand rather far apart here, light of the skies plays freely among
their branches and on the ground beneath them. Just north of here grows a small
stand of low young mellyrn, while farther down the slope towering trees loom
high above you.
name: Arching Path

desc:
The small trail runs through the forest, carefully avoiding the trees, almost as
if it had asked the forest's permission before starting on its ardous journey.
High trees arch above the trail, creating a ceiling that filters the light,
letting only a few rays pass through. As the light reaches the ground, it
illuminates the forest, making it spring into colour and life. Among the
splendid scenery you hear soft singing and voices coming from the south.
name: Trail through the Golden Forest

desc:
You find yourself on the eastern slope of the hill of Caras Galadhon. Through
the surrounding trees you hear song and laughter and the occasional warbling of
a bird. Where the forest opens up towards the north and east, a small curving
path makes its way through the trees. From the south you can hear the quiet
burbling of a small stream.
name: Golden Forest

desc:
The path makes a turn here, bending towards the east and north. As the forest is
rather spacious, it can run in an almost straight line along the slope of the
hill, only avoiding an occasional golden tree. Very soft splashing noises can be
heard in the distant south, mingling with soft singing coming from the east. As
the forest is not so dense here, the light cast by the skies creates a vivid
display of many colours.
name: Curving Path

desc:
Flowing gently down the slope, heading southeast, the small stream seems almost
to be alive. The soft whispers of the trees are answered by the soft splashing
noises of the sparkling water. Very faintly, almost too low to hear, the stream
seems to sing and laugh, teasing you as it travels from the great fountain to
the northwest on its long journey to meet the oceans.
name: A Small Stream through the Lawn

desc:
The large hill that is Caras Galadhon is crowned by a lush green lawn with a
great mallorn reaching high into the sky. The trunk is so thick that it would
take a small army to encircle it. A small stair runs up the trunk to the lower
levels of the flet that can barely be discerned through the colourful branches.
Looking around, the view is splendid, although the surrounding forest blocks
most of it. Still you can gaze over the golden forest and see Galadriel's Garden
as well as the sparkling waters flowing down the hill. Just north of the mallorn
lies a white fountain that sparkles and splashes. Beside it lies a small
pavillion, its glass windows flashing a myriad of colours towards you.
name: At the Home of Celeborn and Galadriel

desc:
Surrounded by grass, the white marble fountain is standing in front of the great
mallorn that is the home of Celeborn and Galadriel. Shimmering and sparkling as
the flowing water catches the light, the fountain creates a veil of soft,
soothing splendour. The faint noise of water splashing down in the small marble
pool surrounding the fountain is relaxing. The combined effect of the sounds of
the pool and the fountain creates a sense of tranquility, as well as deep joy. A
small stream leaves the pool, slowly making its way southeast down the slope,
beginning the long arduous journey towards the mighty river Anduin.
name: The Shimmering Fountain

desc:
You find yourself high on the western slope just below the crest of the hill,
where anyone would feel small in comparison to the towering mallorn rising to
the east. It is by far the largest tree within the city walls. The surrounding
trees seem to shrink, unable to match the uncrowned king of the mellyrn.
name: Golden Forest

desc:
Here, on the western slope just below the crest of the hill, tall trees rise
high towards the skies. Just off the path the undergrowth is still not very
dense, and the ceiling of leaves allows the light of heavens to filter down to
set off dancing shadows within the woods.
name: Golden Forest

desc:
Working its way up the slope, the winding path climbs slowly towards the top of
the hill. Bordered on all sides by the towering trees, the path passes beneath
arches of green, softly whispering branches that filter the faint light of sun,
moon, or stars reaching through them to the ground.
name: The Winding Path

desc:
Having climbed almost halfway up the western slope of the large hill, the
twisting path carefully skirts the large trees as it darts in and out between
the massive trunks. As the trail curves along the slope, it makes a rather sharp
turn and vanishes behind the hill just to the north.
name: The Winding Path

desc:
From atop the great green wall encircling the fair city of Caras Galadhon you
have an excellent view of the golden forest of Lothlorien lying at your feet.
Its vast expanse of trees reaches for the horizon, undisturbed in its might and
beauty.
name: Eastern Wall

desc:
High upon the slightly curving wall that rises above the golden forest, you get
a view that is best described as magnificent and breathtaking. The forest
stretches out below you towards the horizon, only disturbed close to the wall by
a small silvery band trailing southward and by the deep fosse surrounding the
wall.
name: Eastern Wall

desc:
Almost right above the assembly of the bards, the air is filled with sweet music
that makes the post on the eastern wall more a reward than a duty. The high wall
faces due east here, falling off sharply towards the deep fosse before the green
and golden forest reaching out towards the horizon.
name: Eastern Wall

desc:
Facing due east, you are perched on top of the great defensive walls of Caras
Galadhon. Just below the wall, the trees thin out as the ground sinks into the
broad and deep fosse that lies in shadows just beneath the wall. Lifting the
gaze, one can watch the forest stretch endlessly towards the eastern horizon.
name: Eastern Wall

desc:
Finding yourself on top of the green wall you have an excellent view. Behind
you, the large hill that is Caras Galadhon rises, and below you, the shadowed
fosse lies in grey shadow, spanned by a shining white bridge. You can see the
great gates of the proud elven city by your feet. A ladder leads down to the
ground, and one could take a stroll along the wall as it is broad enough to let
several elves walk abreast.
name: Wall above the Gates

desc:
High up on the broad southern wall, your position allows you to see far away.
Curving slightly, the top of the bulwarks is a path, worn by many feet of the
elven guards walking the rounds. The golden forest stretches out for miles and
miles in front of you, interrupted only by the broad shining silver band of the
Celebrant in the distance. Behind you lies the city of Caras Galadhon in
splendour and radiant beauty.
name: Southern Wall

desc:
Curving ever so slightly, the wall still faces mostly southward, giving you a
splendid view above the surrounding forest and the deep shadowed fosse. To the
north rises the green tree-covered hill that is the heart of Lorien, and just by
the foot of the hill lies the enchanted garden of Galadriel.
name: Southern Wall

desc:
This small platform has been built close to the gate to allow archers a better
firing position. On the outside it is walled to shield the elves, and there are
small apertures to allow free line of sight. Just west of the platform the wall
runs above the great gates and to the east it slopes downward. A small circular
tower stands on the wall just above the gates, its smooth surface reflecting
light in all directions.
name: On a High Platform

desc:
Above the wall, a large platform lies nested in the greenery, carefully crafted
to blend into the surroundings. The hidden position allows any defender to send
a storm of lethal arrows raining down onto anyone trying to breach the great
gates of the city. At this elevated position, a small tower was built, reaching
skywards to allow an unsurpassed view of the approaches to the city. Looking
almost due southwest, you can see far past the gate in the green wall
surrounding the large hill of Caras Galadhon. Directly below you, spanning the
shadowed fosse, lies a sparkling stone bridge, leading towards the great gates
of the city. Beyond the bridge stretches the great body of the mallorn forest,
the enchanted Golden Wood of Lothlorien.
name: Above the Gate

desc:
High above the ground, this elevated platform has been built to allow the
defenders of Caras Galadhon to wreak havoc on any advancing invaders. Crafted to
blend in with the hill and the wall, the platform is surrounded by finely made
ornamented walls built to shield the archers, both from view and from any
incoming fire. Looking away from the high platform, slightly to the southwest
you can make out several details in the surrounding woods: a towering mallorn,
the deep shadowed fosse and the shining white bridge spanning it, the graceful
white stone road running in a circle around the wall and the broad silver band
of the Celebrant in the distance.
name: On a High Platform

desc:
This small tower stands directly above the gate of Caras Galadhon. A few narrow
open windows are placed waist-high in the circular wall at even distances to
allow archers to watch and fire upon an approaching foe. Even though it is built
for the purpose of defence, it is richly decorated with carvings in the wood and
silver lamps to light the dark.
name: The Tower

desc:
From this high ledge on the green wall you can see the great gates of the city
of the Galadhrim just below. The large forested hill of Caras Galadhon is due
north of you, and to the south a deep fosse lies below your feet, only spanned
by a sparkling white bridge. You can make out the forest road leading due south
as well as the white stone road that runs around the city.
name: Wall above the Gates

desc:
This oddly-shaped flet placed within the confines of a particularly sprawling
mallorn tree is the home of an elven family. Fresh flowers are strewn on the
floor. A stunning tablecloth of intricate elven weave covers a square knee-high
table in the corner. A ladder is secured on one side of the flet and a length of
elven rope on another, ending in a swing.
name: A Lonely Flet

desc:
Completing its graceful arc, the paved stone road passes below the high green
wall that encircles the bright city of the Galadhrim. Carefully laid, the white
polished stones gleam and shine as they reflect every ray of light that drops on
them, creating a dazzling pattern of light and movement.
name: A White Stone Road

desc:
Steadily anchored among the branches of this great mallorn, the large wooden
flet has been painted white by its inhabitants. Bordered by a low railing, the
platform is large enough for a few families. A large round table stands in the
middle. A few sleeping mats lie on the floor and a few benches stand along the
edges of this spartanly furnished home of the Galadhrim.
name: A White Flet

desc:
This large wooden platform rests firmly among the branches of a great mallorn.
It is bordered by a low wooden railing, painted in gold and green flowing in
intricate patterns. The platform is covered by sleeping mats and wooden benches.
At the trunk of the tree there is a white ladder running down to the ground
level.
name: A Large Flet

desc:
A soft bed and walls hung with instruments are what first meets one's eye.
Taking a closer look reveals several adornments along the walls and a littered
table in a corner. The soft winds blowing through the forest create faint yet
audible harmonies as they sweep through the room, touching every instrument.
Sparkling softly as they move, the instruments reflect the light of a small
silver lamp hanging from a branch. A narrow ladder reaches higher up into the
tree, to let the bards watch the sky and the stars.
name: Flet of the Bards

desc:
This proud mallorn stands higher than most of the trees that surround it. As you
look out over the golden forest of Lothlorien, your gaze is met by an endless
expanse of proud mellyrn, reaching as far as the eye can see.
name: High in the Tree

desc:
In daytime, shadows are cast here and dispelled, never keeping the same shape
for more than a few seconds as the bridge sparkles and sends light shimmering
across the water surface. Although in a permanent state of dusk, the shadows
below the bridge are friendly and joyful, never hostile. The quiet trickle of
the stream creates a soothing background as the arch of the bridge enhances and
magnifies the sounds of the lively water.
name: In the Shadow of a Bridge

desc:
Here below the beehives kept by the elves a large colony of bees has created a
large hive for themselves where they can exist undisturbed. Not as orderly as
the hives above, this earthen dwelling still can contain a large amount of the
buzzing creatures and the golden treasure they create - honey!
name: Inside a Large Beehive

desc:
By climbing out upon this broad, silver-skinned branch, it is possible to reach
a point within the mallorn tree-top from which one can see for several miles to
the east and south. The bulk of the tree lies north and west of this lofty
perch, so that visibility in those directions is completely blocked by the
foliage. In the east, the mighty Anduin flows through several wide bends,
marking one border of Lorien. Beyond this point, a green, forested ridge rises
to a rocky peak and follows the river's north-south course. Away to the south,
the mallorn-trees intermingle with smaller, deciduous varieties; a trail may be
seen leading away in that direction, out of the elven lands.
name: Overhanging Branch

desc:
Built high above the ground, deep within an ancient mallorn treetop, this large
flet is surrounded by magnificent colours that bring to mind the treasure hoard
of a mighty dragon of legend. Sturdy tree-limbs of smooth, unflawed silver
support thick bunches of leaves that glitter and shine as they sway in the
breeze. Built of wooden planks lashed firmly together by several lengths of thin
cordage, this structure has only two walls, above which is stretched a green,
woven tarp. It appears that the walls are portable and may be moved around,
depending on the wind direction.
name: House of Gold and Silver

desc:
A small, irregularly-shaped platform has been built into the crook of two thick
mallorn-boughs and the smooth, silver trunk that connects them. The structure
seems to be designed as a waypoint from which it is possible to continue
climbing up into the mighty tree; to this purpose, a slender rope ladder extends
up into the golden tree-crown. The sound of soft, musical voices can barely be
heard from that direction. Below, the trunk plunges deeply into the forest's
earth floor; many more mellyrn grow nearby, so that visibility is hindered at
this level by their dense foliage.
name: Narrow Platform

desc:
In days immemorial the Sea must have covered this land, many layers of sediment
settling on the bottom, allowing limestone to form where later the landscape
changed and changed until it was finally overgrown by a forest. Many hundreds of
miles away from the nearest sea shore, in a now landlocked terrain, fossils and
shells of sea-creatures decorate this cavern, most of them broken and crumbled,
but some preserved in the limestone almost intact. Still, as the sound of
running water reaches here and bounces among the walls, one can almost imagine
that it is the sound of the Sea beating at the walls of a harbour.
name: Limestone Cavern

desc:
A tiny iridescent stream of pure water bubbles out of the ground and over a few
haphazardly laid stones. The grass-covered ground rises on either side of it -
enough to make sure no one would get their feet wet while collecting water. The
spring is concealed within the shadow of a few very tall mellyrn, whose
reflection gives the spring water its opalescent glow. The golden forest hinders
the view in all directions.
name: Shadowed Spring

desc:
High in the crown of a tall elm rests this light wooden platform, gently held by
the great tree on the tips of its branches. A faint silvery glimmer covers the
flet and the branches above it like a transparent dome woven of spider webs. An
elaborately carved railing runs around the flet, and a grey ladder leads down
through an opening by the tree trunk.
name: On a Grey Flet

desc:
As a sign of their hospitality, the Galadhrim have built this small pavilion on
the ground, well aware of the fact that many of their visitors are unable to
find peace of mind sleeping up in the trees. The pavilion stands just east of a
marvellous fountain close to the foot of the house of Celeborn in the centre of
Caras Galadhon. Built of the finest wood covered with intricate carvings, with
windows made of coloured glass, the pavilion is a work of art, worthy of any
ruler.
name: A Wooden Pavilion

desc:
This chamber hewn in the rock has many small alcoves carved in the walls. Dried
herbs stashed in them produce a soft aromatic smell that permeates this place. A
tall countertop, with three sets of scales in different sizes, lines the north
wall. Oak barrels crowd the west corner of the room far from the doors. A set of
smoothly polished wooden beams serves as the ceiling.
name: Dry Chamber
